channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Internet Law and Policy Foundry (ILPF) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2028 Class 8 Fellowship Program. Designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of law, technology, policy, governance, and digital innovation, this two-year professional development fellowship provides an exceptional platform for participants to expand their expertise, grow their networks, and […]

The post Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Internet Law and Policy Foundry (ILPF) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive 2026–2028 Class 8 Fellowship Program. Designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of law, technology, policy, governance, and digital innovation, this two-year professional development fellowship provides an exceptional platform for participants to expand their expertise, grow their networks, and contribute meaningfully to conversations shaping the future of the internet.

As governments, companies, civil society organizations, and international institutions continue to grapple with rapidly evolving technological challenges, there is an increasing need for informed professionals who can bridge the gap between technology and public policy. The Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship aims to cultivate exactly this type of leadership.

The fellowship welcomes applications from students, researchers, legal professionals, policy practitioners, academics, advocates, and technology professionals from around the world who are passionate about internet governance, digital rights, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, privacy, online safety, telecommunications, and related fields.

About the Internet Law and Policy Foundry

The Internet Law and Policy Foundry is a professional development organization dedicated to fostering the next generation of leaders in internet law and technology policy. Through mentorship, networking opportunities, collaborative projects, events, publications, and committee participation, the Foundry creates an environment where diverse perspectives can engage in constructive dialogue and contribute to solving pressing digital policy challenges.

One of the organization’s core objectives is to identify talented individuals from various professional, academic, and geographic backgrounds and help them develop into influential voices within the broader technology policy ecosystem.

The Foundry actively encourages participation from individuals with diverse experiences, viewpoints, and professional trajectories, recognizing that effective internet governance requires inclusive and multidisciplinary collaboration.

Fellowship Structure

The Foundry Fellowship is a two-year unpaid professional development program.

While the fellowship is largely conducted remotely, participants typically gather in person with other fellows in their region whenever opportunities arise. Fellows may also participate in larger technology policy events and conferences, including opportunities to engage with the wider Foundry community at major industry gatherings such as the State of the Net Conference.

Junior Fellows are expected to contribute approximately 3–5 hours per week toward fellowship activities, programming, and committee responsibilities.

Although there is no residency requirement, applicants should be aware that most Foundry activities occur during North American business hours, particularly Eastern Time (ET). Successful applicants should therefore be prepared to contribute during those time periods.

Committee Opportunities

All applicants are required to rank their preferred committee assignments. These committees play a critical role in advancing the Foundry’s mission and provide fellows with valuable leadership and professional development experiences.

Tech Policy Grind Podcast Committee

This committee manages the Foundry’s podcast production and content development. Members help identify discussion topics, coordinate guest speakers, develop episode concepts, and collaborate on outreach efforts. Participants gain hands-on experience in media production, thought leadership, stakeholder engagement, and policy communications.

Events & Fundraising Committee

The Events & Fundraising Committee is responsible for planning and executing many of the Foundry’s flagship programs and networking opportunities. Members help organize conferences, professional events, hackathons, social gatherings, sponsorship activities, and other initiatives that bring together the technology policy community.

Editorial & Content Committee

This committee develops original written content for publication. Fellows contribute articles, policy analyses, legal commentary, opinion pieces, case summaries, technical handbooks, policy guides, and other thought leadership materials. This committee offers an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking to strengthen their research, writing, and policy communication skills.

Communications & Marketing Committee

Members of this committee support the Foundry’s outreach efforts, newsletter development, branding initiatives, social media engagement, website management, and professional communications. Fellows gain practical experience in strategic communications while helping amplify the work of the Foundry community.

Why Apply?

The Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to become part of a vibrant network of professionals shaping the future of technology governance.

Benefits include:

  • Access to a global network of technology policy professionals
  • Professional development and leadership opportunities
  • Collaboration with experts across law, government, academia, civil society, and industry
  • Opportunities to publish original work
  • Participation in technology policy events and conferences
  • Development of practical skills in communications, policy analysis, event management, research, and advocacy
  • Exposure to emerging issues in internet governance and technology regulation
  • Long-term career development support

Areas of Interest

The fellowship is particularly relevant for individuals interested in:

  • Artificial Intelligence Governance
  • Internet Governance
  • Cybersecurity Policy
  • Data Privacy and Protection
  • Platform Accountability
  • Digital Rights
  • Telecommunications Policy
  • Content Moderation
  • Online Safety
  • Technology Regulation
  • Digital Trade
  • Emerging Technology Law

Application Timeline

Applications Open: June 1, 2026

Application Deadline: June 22, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time (ET)

Selection Results: Mid-July 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submissions are strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form, submit their resume or CV in PDF format, rank their committee preferences, and respond to several written essay questions focused on technology policy, leadership, diversity, and professional interests.

Applicants will also be asked to discuss how their experiences and perspectives can contribute to the Foundry community and advance diversity and inclusion within the internet law and policy field.

For professionals and students seeking meaningful engagement in technology governance, policy development, and digital innovation, the Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship represents a valuable opportunity to build expertise, expand professional networks, and contribute to shaping the future of the digital world.

Deadline: June 22, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

The post Internet Law and Policy Foundry Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Detailed Guide to the Prestigious Global Professional Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at University College London (UCL) present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded doctoral study in anthropology. Offered through UCL Anthropology and supported by the generous funding of the Sigrid Rausing Trust, these prestigious scholarships are designed to support outstanding scholars pursuing innovative and impactful research at […]

The post Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at University College London (UCL) present an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded doctoral study in anthropology. Offered through UCL Anthropology and supported by the generous funding of the Sigrid Rausing Trust, these prestigious scholarships are designed to support outstanding scholars pursuing innovative and impactful research at one of the world’s leading anthropology departments.

Named in honor of the renowned anthropologist Mary Douglas, whose groundbreaking work transformed the understanding of culture, risk, symbolism, and social institutions, the scholarship aims to continue her legacy by supporting the next generation of anthropological researchers.

Applications are now open for students wishing to commence their doctoral studies in September 2026, with successful candidates joining UCL’s vibrant academic community and benefiting from world-class supervision, interdisciplinary collaboration, and extensive research resources.

About the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships

UCL Anthropology has announced two fully funded Mary Douglas Research Scholarships for doctoral candidates beginning their PhD journey in 2026. The scholarships are intended to support highly motivated researchers who demonstrate strong academic potential and propose original research projects capable of contributing significantly to anthropological knowledge.

As one of the most respected anthropology departments globally, UCL offers an intellectually stimulating environment where students engage with leading academics and researchers across a wide range of anthropological disciplines.

The scholarship recognizes academic excellence, originality of thought, and the potential of applicants to make meaningful contributions to the field of anthropology.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful candidates will receive comprehensive financial support for a period of three years.

The funding package includes:

  • Full coverage of Home (UK) tuition fees.
  • A maintenance stipend provided at UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) minimum rates.
  • Access to UCL’s extensive academic resources and research facilities.
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration across departments and research centers.
  • Supervision from internationally recognized scholars in anthropology.
  • Membership in one of the world’s most prestigious research communities.

The scholarship significantly reduces financial barriers, allowing students to focus fully on their research and academic development.

Research Areas Supported

Applications are welcomed across the broad spectrum of anthropological research. UCL encourages proposals in areas including:

  • Social Anthropology
  • Cultural Anthropology
  • Medical Anthropology
  • Digital Anthropology
  • Visual Anthropology
  • Evolutionary Anthropology
  • Environmental Anthropology
  • Material Culture Studies
  • Interdisciplinary Anthropological Research

Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative projects that demonstrate originality, relevance, and methodological rigor.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026, applicants must:

  • Meet UCL’s academic requirements for admission to a PhD programme in Anthropology.
  • Demonstrate strong academic achievement.
  • Submit a compelling and original research proposal.
  • Show evidence of research potential and scholarly excellence.

The scholarship is primarily intended for applicants who qualify for Home (UK) tuition fee status.

However, overseas applicants are also welcome to apply. It is important to note that scholarship funding for tuition fees is capped at the Home fee level. Therefore, international students who are classified as overseas fee-paying students may need to secure additional funding to cover any difference between Home and Overseas tuition rates.

Fee status will be determined according to UCL’s standard procedures and relevant UK government regulations.

Application Documents Required

Applicants must submit a complete PhD application package, which includes:

1. Research Proposal

A maximum one-page research proposal outlining:

  • Research objectives
  • Originality of the project
  • Methodology
  • Feasibility of the study

Applicants should clearly indicate that they wish to be considered for the Mary Douglas Scholarship.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A maximum three-page CV highlighting:

  • Academic achievements
  • Research experience
  • Conference presentations
  • Peer-reviewed publications
  • Relevant professional experience

3. Academic Transcripts

Applicants must provide undergraduate transcripts and postgraduate transcripts where applicable.

4. Supervisor Support Letter

A letter of support from a prospective supervisor within UCL’s Anthropology Department.

5. Academic References

Names and contact details of two academic referees who can comment on the applicant’s academic ability and research potential.

6. Personal Statement

A personal statement of up to 500 words describing:

  • Why the applicant meets the scholarship criteria.
  • Relevant academic and professional experiences.
  • Motivation for pursuing doctoral research.
  • Future academic and career aspirations.

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated primarily on:

  • Academic merit.
  • Quality and originality of the proposed research project.
  • Feasibility of the research.
  • Research potential of the candidate.
  • Strength of supporting documents and references.

Competition for these scholarships is expected to be highly competitive, making it essential for applicants to submit well-developed and compelling applications.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their complete application package to UCL Anthropology.

Applications and inquiries should be sent via email.

Applicants must include the subject line:

“Mary Douglas Scholarship”

Why Apply for the Mary Douglas Research Scholarships?

The Mary Douglas Research Scholarships offer far more than financial support. They provide an opportunity to study at one of the world’s leading universities, work alongside distinguished anthropologists, and contribute original research to pressing global conversations.

For students passionate about understanding human societies, cultures, health systems, environments, technologies, and social change, this scholarship offers an outstanding platform to launch a successful academic and research career.

By joining UCL Anthropology, scholars become part of a dynamic intellectual community dedicated to advancing anthropological knowledge and addressing contemporary global challenges through rigorous research and innovative thinking.

Important Deadline 

16 June 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Mary Douglas Research Scholarships 2026 at UCL: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Anthropology in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Young innovators, entrepreneurs, organizations, and change-makers working in the bioeconomy sector now have an exciting opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to shaping future global policies. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Assembly, in collaboration with the Office of Climate Change, Biodiversity and Environment of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations […]

The post World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young innovators, entrepreneurs, organizations, and change-makers working in the bioeconomy sector now have an exciting opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to shaping future global policies. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Assembly, in collaboration with the Office of Climate Change, Biodiversity and Environment of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), has officially launched a global call for submissions to identify and showcase youth-led bioeconomy projects that are transforming agrifood systems around the world.

This initiative seeks to amplify youth voices, highlight innovative solutions, and gather evidence-based insights that will contribute to future policy recommendations for governments, international organizations, and key stakeholders involved in agrifood systems transformation.

About the Opportunity

The World Food Forum is a global platform hosted by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). It works to accelerate progress toward sustainable development by engaging youth, innovators, researchers, policymakers, investors, and communities in creating solutions for global food and agricultural challenges.

Through this call for submissions, the WFF Youth Assembly aims to identify and document successful youth-led bioeconomy initiatives that are contributing to more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive agrifood systems.

The initiative recognizes that young people are playing a critical role in developing innovative approaches to agriculture, forestry, fisheries, aquaculture, biodiversity conservation, and related sectors. By gathering information about these projects, the organizers hope to strengthen youth participation in policy discussions and create recommendations that influence decision-making at national, regional, and international levels.

Objectives of the Call

The call for submissions has several important objectives:

1. Highlight Youth Contributions

The initiative seeks to showcase the contributions of young people, especially young women, who are advancing bioeconomy solutions that support sustainable agrifood systems transformation.

2. Promote Youth-Led Innovation

The organizers want to identify and celebrate projects that create employment opportunities, strengthen local economies, improve livelihoods, and generate positive community impacts.

3. Identify Challenges and Barriers

The survey will help uncover challenges faced by young people in accessing:

  • Finance and investment opportunities
  • Capacity development programs
  • Technical support and training
  • Resources and infrastructure
  • Decision-making platforms and policy spaces

Understanding these barriers will help inform future recommendations that support youth engagement in the bioeconomy sector.

What Is Bioeconomy?

Bioeconomy refers to the sustainable production, utilization, and conservation of biological resources to create products, services, and economic value while protecting the environment.

Examples include:

  • Sustainable agriculture practices
  • Circular economy initiatives
  • Renewable biological resources
  • Agricultural biotechnology
  • Biodiversity conservation projects
  • Sustainable fisheries and aquaculture
  • Bio-based products and innovations
  • Waste-to-resource solutions
  • Climate-smart agricultural technologies

Projects operating in these areas are encouraged to apply.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The information collected through this global initiative will not simply remain in a report. Instead, the findings will contribute directly to the development of youth-led policy recommendations that will be presented to decision-makers at multiple levels.

These recommendations will inform discussions within FAO Technical Committees on:

  • Agriculture
  • Forestry
  • Fisheries
  • Aquaculture

The findings may also contribute to discussions at future Global Bioeconomy Summits and other international policy platforms.

This means participants have an opportunity to influence conversations that shape the future of agrifood systems worldwide.

Who Can Apply?

The call is open to:

  • Young people aged 18–35
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Youth-led companies
  • Youth collectives
  • Youth initiatives
  • Youth movements
  • Formal and informal youth groups

Applicants from every region of the world are encouraged to participate.

Eligible Projects

Projects and initiatives must:

  • Demonstrate measurable impact
  • Showcase innovation
  • Have potential for scalability and replication
  • Focus on agrifood systems transformation
  • Be led by young people or youth-led entities

Relevant sectors include:

  • Agriculture
  • Forestry
  • Fisheries
  • Aquaculture
  • Biodiversity
  • One Health
  • Agrifood value chains
  • Climate-smart agriculture
  • Sustainable food systems

What Information Will Be Collected?

Participants will complete a global survey designed to gather both quantitative and qualitative information.

The survey will assess:

  • Project impact
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Scalability potential
  • Job creation outcomes
  • Income generation opportunities
  • Success factors
  • Challenges and barriers to implementation

The information collected will help build a stronger evidence base for youth engagement in bioeconomy development.

What Happens After Submission?

Following the survey process, participants will be invited to participate in an online consultation.

During this consultation, participants will:

  • Review preliminary findings
  • Validate emerging insights
  • Discuss priorities for action
  • Help shape youth-led policy recommendations

These recommendations will then be presented by youth representatives during international forums and policy discussions involving governments, international organizations, and other stakeholders.

Language Requirements

Submissions are accepted in all official United Nations languages:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • English
  • French
  • Russian
  • Spanish

This ensures broad global participation and inclusivity.

Benefits of Participating

Participants stand to gain several advantages, including:

  • International visibility for their projects
  • Recognition within global agrifood and bioeconomy networks
  • Opportunity to contribute to global policy recommendations
  • Engagement with international experts and stakeholders
  • Participation in consultation processes
  • Networking opportunities with youth leaders worldwide
  • Contribution to future Global Bioeconomy Summit discussions
  • Increased exposure for innovative youth-led initiatives

Application Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their projects no later than 1 July 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post World Food Forum 2026 Call for Submissions: Global Opportunity for Youth-Led Bioeconomy Projects Driving Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Global Health Politics Workshop at Boston University is now accepting applications for the 2026–2027 Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship. Sponsored by the Pardee Center for the Study of the Longer-Range Future, this prestigious fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity for doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics to engage with leading scholars, receive mentorship, expand […]

The post Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Health Politics Workshop at Boston University is now accepting applications for the 2026–2027 Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship. Sponsored by the Pardee Center for the Study of the Longer-Range Future, this prestigious fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity for doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics to engage with leading scholars, receive mentorship, expand their professional networks, and contribute to critical discussions shaping the future of global health policy.

Applications are open until July 3, 2026, at 8:00 PM EST, and selected fellows will participate in a one-year academic engagement program designed to strengthen scholarship and collaboration in the growing field of global health politics.

About the Global Health Politics Workshop

The Global Health Politics Workshop serves as an interdisciplinary academic platform dedicated to understanding how political, social, and institutional factors influence health outcomes worldwide. The workshop explores how research in global health politics can contribute to addressing some of the world’s most pressing health challenges.

The program focuses on major issues including:

  • Viral pandemics and global preparedness
  • Non-communicable diseases
  • Antimicrobial resistance
  • Universal health coverage
  • Global health governance
  • Power inequalities between the Global North and Global South
  • International health policy and decision-making

By bringing together scholars from diverse backgrounds, the workshop creates opportunities for meaningful dialogue, collaboration, and intellectual exchange on topics that affect populations across the globe.

What Makes This Fellowship Unique?

Unlike many academic fellowships that require relocation, the Global Health Politics Fellowship is entirely non-residential. Participants are not required to move to Boston or reside near the university.

Instead, the fellowship leverages virtual technology to connect fellows with faculty members, workshop participants, guest speakers, and members of the Steering Committee. This format allows scholars from around the world to participate while maintaining their existing academic or professional commitments.

The fellowship is particularly valuable for emerging scholars seeking to build academic visibility and strengthen their research within an international network of experts.

Fellowship Responsibilities

Selected fellows will become active members of the Global Health Politics Workshop community during the 2026–2027 academic year.

Fellows are expected to:

  • Attend all eight workshop talks held throughout the academic year.
  • Participate in four workshop sessions each semester.
  • Join either virtually through Zoom or attend in person if based in Boston.
  • Engage actively in workshop discussions and question-and-answer sessions.
  • Participate in optional one-on-one meetings with visiting speakers.
  • Receive mentorship and constructive feedback on ongoing research projects.
  • Collaborate and engage with other fellows and members of the Steering Committee.
  • Contribute to intellectual discussions surrounding contemporary global health issues.

These responsibilities are designed not only to deepen participants’ knowledge but also to create opportunities for professional growth and long-term scholarly collaboration.

Fellowship Benefits

The fellowship offers a variety of academic and professional benefits that can significantly enhance a scholar’s career trajectory.

Selected fellows will receive:

Access to a Prestigious Academic Network

Participants will join a vibrant community of researchers and practitioners working in global health politics. This network can facilitate future collaborations, publications, and career opportunities.

Mentorship from Leading Scholars

Fellows will receive guidance and feedback from experienced faculty members and experts in the field, helping them refine research projects and strengthen academic contributions.

Engagement with Distinguished Speakers

The workshop regularly hosts guest speakers and leading researchers whose expertise spans multiple dimensions of global health policy and politics.

Increased Professional Visibility

Participation in the fellowship can enhance fellows’ academic profiles by providing opportunities to present ideas, discuss research, and engage with influential scholars.

Research Development Support

Constructive feedback from mentors and peers can help fellows strengthen manuscripts, dissertation chapters, policy analyses, and other scholarly work.

Modest Financial Support

Fellows will receive a stipend of approximately $350.

Please note that participants located in the United States on certain visa categories may only receive the stipend if they possess legal authorization to do so.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy one of the following academic categories:

  • Current PhD students in good standing
  • Postdoctoral fellows conducting research related to global health politics
  • First-year professors

Applicants should also demonstrate a strong interest in the politics of global health, broadly defined.

The program welcomes interdisciplinary perspectives and encourages applications from scholars whose research engages with political, social, governance, policy, or institutional dimensions of health.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

1. Cover Letter (PDF)

The cover letter should include:

  • One paragraph explaining why you wish to be selected as a Global Health Politics Fellow.
  • One to two paragraphs describing your research interests.
  • An explanation of how participation in the fellowship aligns with your broader academic and career goals.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applicants must submit an up-to-date CV outlining their educational background, research experience, publications, presentations, and other relevant achievements.

Important Application Notice

Applicants should carefully prepare original application materials.

The fellowship announcement specifically notes that applications generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI) will not be considered during the selection process.

Applications should be submitted directly to: Joseph Harris Director, Global Health Politics Workshop.

Selection Timeline

The selection process will proceed according to the following schedule:

  • July 2026 to early August 2026: Application review period
  • Mid-to-late August 2026: Notification of selected fellows

Successful applicants will then join the workshop for the 2026–2027 academic year.

Why You Should Apply

For doctoral students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academics working in global health politics, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with leading experts while remaining based anywhere in the world. The combination of mentorship, scholarly networking, research feedback, and professional development makes this fellowship an excellent platform for advancing both academic careers and contributions to global health policy.

As global health challenges continue to grow in complexity, opportunities such as the Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship play an important role in supporting the next generation of scholars dedicated to improving health outcomes and governance worldwide.

Application Deadline: July 3, 2026 (8:00 PM EST)

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

The post Boston University Non-Resident Global Health Politics Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Explained Guide for PhD Students, Postdocs, and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Chapin Hall Careers 2026: Join a Leading Child and Family Policy Research Organization with Remote Work Opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Finding a meaningful career that combines research, public policy, social impact, and professional growth can be challenging. For professionals seeking to create lasting change in the lives of children, families, and communities, Chapin Hall offers a unique opportunity to contribute to evidence-based solutions that influence policy and practice across the United States. Chapin Hall is […]

The post Chapin Hall Careers 2026: Join a Leading Child and Family Policy Research Organization with Remote Work Opportunity. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Finding a meaningful career that combines research, public policy, social impact, and professional growth can be challenging. For professionals seeking to create lasting change in the lives of children, families, and communities, Chapin Hall offers a unique opportunity to contribute to evidence-based solutions that influence policy and practice across the United States.

Chapin Hall is an independent policy research center affiliated with the University of Chicago. Since its establishment, the organization has built a strong reputation for producing rigorous research, practical tools, and policy recommendations that improve child welfare systems, youth development initiatives, community support structures, and family well-being.

Through its fully remote work model, commitment to diversity and inclusion, and investment in employee development, Chapin Hall has become an attractive employer for professionals interested in research, policy analysis, data science, public health, social work, and related disciplines.

About Chapin Hall

Chapin Hall is dedicated to helping children, youth, families, and communities thrive. The organization works closely with policymakers, practitioners, government agencies, nonprofits, and philanthropic organizations to generate actionable research and practical solutions to some of society’s most pressing challenges.

The institution’s work spans several critical areas, including:

  • Child welfare systems
  • Youth homelessness
  • Family support services
  • Community capacity building
  • Public policy development
  • Program evaluation
  • Data analytics and research
  • Social service system improvement

The organization brings together multidisciplinary teams consisting of researchers, policy analysts, data scientists, communications professionals, subject matter experts, information technology specialists, and administrative staff. These teams collaborate to solve complex social issues through evidence-based approaches.

Why Work at Chapin Hall?

A Mission-Driven Career

One of the most compelling reasons to work at Chapin Hall is the opportunity to contribute to meaningful social change. Employees are not simply performing routine job functions; they are participating in projects that help improve outcomes for vulnerable children, youth, and families.

Whether working in research, policy, analytics, communications, or operations, every employee contributes to the organization’s broader mission of advancing child and family well-being.

Fully Remote Work Environment

Chapin Hall has embraced a comprehensive Work From Anywhere policy. Although the organization was founded in Chicago, employees now work from various locations across the United States.

This flexible model allows professionals to:

  • Maintain a healthy work-life balance
  • Work from locations that suit their personal circumstances
  • Access opportunities regardless of geographic location
  • Collaborate with diverse colleagues from across the country
  • Participate in impactful national projects without relocating

The organization also invests heavily in technology and infrastructure to ensure employees can effectively collaborate and perform their duties remotely.

Strong Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

Chapin Hall believes that diversity strengthens research, policymaking, and organizational effectiveness.

The organization actively seeks to recruit, develop, and retain individuals from diverse backgrounds. It values differences in race, ethnicity, gender identity, religion, age, sexual orientation, ability, and lived experiences.

Particularly noteworthy is Chapin Hall’s commitment to including individuals whose experiences reflect the realities faced by families and young people served by child- and family-focused systems.

This commitment to equity extends beyond hiring practices and is reflected in research methodologies, policy recommendations, organizational culture, and partnerships.

Employee Benefits at Chapin Hall

Comprehensive Benefits Package

Chapin Hall recognizes that employee well-being is essential to organizational success. As a result, employees have access to a robust package of benefits designed to support both professional and personal growth.

Paid Parental Leave

The organization offers a generous 12-week paid parental leave program. This benefit applies to parents welcoming children through:

  • Birth
  • Adoption
  • Court-ordered custody placements
  • Surrogacy

The program allows employees to focus on family bonding during important life transitions.

Generous Paid Time Off

Employees receive substantial paid leave benefits that help support personal well-being, rest, family responsibilities, and work-life balance.

Retirement Benefits

Chapin Hall offers retirement support through a competitive employer matching program, helping employees prepare for long-term financial security.

Health Insurance Coverage

Comprehensive health insurance options are available to support the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of employees and their families.

Professional Development Opportunities

Professional growth is a core component of the employee experience at Chapin Hall.

Staff members receive annual opportunities for professional development, allowing them to strengthen existing skills, develop new competencies, attend training programs, and advance their careers.

The organization promotes continuous learning and encourages employees to explore innovative approaches that enhance research quality and policy impact.

Building Community in a Remote Workplace

While Chapin Hall operates remotely, the organization understands the importance of personal connection and team collaboration.

To strengthen workplace relationships, the organization hosts:

  • Annual in-person staff convenings
  • Team-building activities
  • Virtual social events
  • Professional learning sessions
  • Community engagement opportunities

Employees participate in at least two multi-day organizational gatherings each year that focus on collaboration, shared learning, and relationship building.

Additionally, the Habitat Committee organizes various activities throughout the year to help foster a sense of belonging and community among staff.

Career Opportunities at Chapin Hall

Chapin Hall regularly recruits professionals for positions such as:

  • Researcher
  • Senior Researcher
  • Policy Analyst
  • Associate Policy Analyst
  • Data Scientist
  • Data Engineer
  • Project Manager
  • Communications Specialist
  • Research Support Staff
  • Administrative Professionals

Many positions require expertise in public policy, social work, sociology, psychology, public health, statistics, economics, data science, program evaluation, or related fields.

Candidates with strong research, analytical, writing, project management, and stakeholder engagement skills are often highly competitive.

Final Thoughts

For professionals seeking a purpose-driven career, Chapin Hall represents a remarkable opportunity to influence policy, improve social systems, and create measurable impact in the lives of children and families. Its combination of mission-focused work, remote flexibility, professional development opportunities, comprehensive benefits, and commitment to equity makes it one of the most attractive employers in the social policy and research sector.

Individuals interested in contributing to evidence-based solutions that shape the future of child and family well-being should explore current opportunities at Chapin Hall and consider joining a team dedicated to meaningful and lasting change.

Deadline:

No specific deadline has been announced. Applications are accepted based on available openings and recruitment needs.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Chapin Hall Careers 2026: Join a Leading Child and Family Policy Research Organization with Remote Work Opportunity. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice Policy Analyst Job Opportunity 2026: Advance Equity, Shape Public Policy, and Drive Meaningful Change in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The New York City Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice (MOERJ) is inviting passionate and qualified professionals to apply for the position of Policy Analyst. This exciting career opportunity offers candidates the chance to contribute directly to equity-centered policymaking and help create a more inclusive, fair, and just New York City. For individuals who […]

The post NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice Policy Analyst Job Opportunity 2026: Advance Equity, Shape Public Policy, and Drive Meaningful Change in New York City. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New York City Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice (MOERJ) is inviting passionate and qualified professionals to apply for the position of Policy Analyst. This exciting career opportunity offers candidates the chance to contribute directly to equity-centered policymaking and help create a more inclusive, fair, and just New York City.

For individuals who are passionate about public service, policy development, social justice, research, and creating systems that improve the lives of communities, this role presents a unique opportunity to work at the heart of one of the largest municipal governments in the world.

About the NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice

The NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice (MOERJ) serves as New York City’s centralized equity office. Established to advance racial equity and social justice across city government, the office works collaboratively with agencies, communities, and stakeholders to develop policies and programs that reduce disparities and improve outcomes for all New Yorkers.

Launched as part of New York City’s commitment to equity and inclusion, MOERJ plays a critical role in implementing citywide racial equity initiatives and ensuring that government policies are developed through an equity-focused lens.

The office brings together several important initiatives and commissions, including:

  • NYC Commission on Gender Equity
  • NYC Her Future
  • NYC Young Men’s Initiative
  • NYC Unity Project
  • NYC Pay Equity Cabinet
  • NYC Taskforce on Racial Inclusion and Equity

Together, these initiatives work toward eliminating systemic barriers and promoting opportunities for historically underserved communities.

About the Policy Analyst Position

The Policy Analyst position is designed for individuals who are eager to contribute to meaningful policy development and implementation. The selected candidate will work closely with internal teams, policy experts, city agencies, and external stakeholders to support the advancement of the office’s policy priorities.

This role is ideal for professionals who enjoy conducting research, analyzing data, evaluating policy impacts, and developing recommendations that can influence large-scale government decisions.

The position offers a chance to be involved in initiatives that directly impact millions of residents across New York City’s five boroughs.

Key Responsibilities

As a Policy Analyst, the successful candidate will be expected to:

Conduct Policy Research

The analyst will perform in-depth research on policy issues related to equity, racial justice, social inclusion, and public administration. This research will help inform decision-making and support the development of evidence-based recommendations.

Analyze Public Policy Issues

Candidates will evaluate existing policies, identify gaps, assess outcomes, and propose improvements that align with the office’s mission and goals.

Collaborate Across Government Agencies

The role requires working with various city departments and agencies to coordinate efforts and ensure that equity principles are integrated into policy planning and implementation.

Engage Policy Experts and Stakeholders

The analyst will interact with subject matter experts, community organizations, advocacy groups, researchers, and policymakers to gather insights and support policy development efforts.

Support Strategic Initiatives

The selected candidate will contribute to special projects, citywide equity plans, and initiatives aimed at advancing social and racial justice throughout New York City.

Develop Reports and Recommendations

Policy analysts will prepare reports, briefs, presentations, and recommendations that help leadership make informed decisions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Public policy has the power to shape communities, influence opportunities, and improve quality of life. Through this role, successful candidates will have the opportunity to contribute to policies that address inequities in areas such as:

  • Education
  • Employment
  • Healthcare
  • Housing
  • Economic opportunity
  • Public services
  • Community development

The work conducted by the Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice directly impacts the lives of millions of residents and helps ensure that city government serves all communities fairly and effectively.

Benefits of Working with MOERJ

Professionals joining the NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice can expect:

  • Meaningful public service impact
  • Opportunity to influence policy at a citywide level
  • Collaboration with leading policy experts
  • Professional growth and development
  • Exposure to high-level government initiatives
  • Diverse and inclusive work environment
  • Competitive compensation package

According to available job information, the salary range for the Policy Analyst position is approximately $75,000 to $84,400 annually. Actual compensation may vary depending on experience and qualifications.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is particularly suitable for:

  • Public Policy Graduates
  • Public Administration Professionals
  • Social Scientists
  • Political Science Graduates
  • Economists
  • Researchers
  • Government Affairs Specialists
  • Social Justice Advocates
  • Nonprofit Policy Professionals
  • Community Development Experts

Candidates with strong analytical, communication, research, and stakeholder engagement skills will be especially competitive.

Candidates are encouraged to apply early, as hiring timelines may vary and positions can close once sufficient applications have been received.

Final Thoughts

The Policy Analyst role at the NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice represents an exceptional opportunity for professionals seeking to combine their passion for policy, research, and public service. By joining MOERJ, successful candidates will contribute to the development of policies that promote fairness, inclusion, and opportunity for millions of New Yorkers.

If you are committed to creating positive social impact, advancing equity, and helping shape the future of one of the world’s most influential cities, this opportunity is worth serious consideration.

Deadline:

No official deadline has been announced. Early application is strongly encouraged.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity , click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post NYC Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice Policy Analyst Job Opportunity 2026: Advance Equity, Shape Public Policy, and Drive Meaningful Change in New York City. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aspen Albright International Leaders Program 2028: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for International Professionals in the United States
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Aspen Institute, through the Aspen Strategy Group, has launched the prestigious Albright International Leaders Program (AILP), a transformative leadership initiative designed to empower and connect outstanding international professionals working in foreign policy and related fields. Established in honor of former United States Secretary of State Madeleine K. Albright, the program seeks to cultivate the […]

The post Aspen Albright International Leaders Program 2028: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for International Professionals in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aspen Institute, through the Aspen Strategy Group, has launched the prestigious Albright International Leaders Program (AILP), a transformative leadership initiative designed to empower and connect outstanding international professionals working in foreign policy and related fields. Established in honor of former United States Secretary of State Madeleine K. Albright, the program seeks to cultivate the next generation of global leaders who are committed to diplomacy, international cooperation, and public service.

The Albright International Leaders Program is a unique year-long leadership experience that brings together a select cohort of accomplished international professionals currently based in the United States. Participants will gain access to world-class leadership development opportunities, influential policymakers, renowned experts, and a lifelong network of global leaders.

Applications for the inaugural Class of 2028 are expected to open in June 2027, with successful candidates beginning the program in the fall of 2027 and graduating at the Aspen Security Forum in July 2028.

About the Albright International Leaders Program

The Albright International Leaders Program was created to honor the remarkable legacy of Madeleine K. Albright, the first female U.S. Secretary of State and one of the most influential diplomats in modern history. The program reflects her commitment to diplomacy, international collaboration, democratic values, and leadership development.

As a complement to the Aspen Strategy Group Rising Leaders Program, which focuses primarily on U.S. professionals, the Albright International Leaders Program specifically serves international professionals working in the United States. It aims to foster meaningful dialogue, strengthen international cooperation, and build lasting relationships among leaders from diverse countries and professional backgrounds.

The initiative is supported by the Aspen Institute and the Aspen Strategy Group, organizations known globally for bringing together leaders, policymakers, academics, journalists, and innovators to address some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

What Participants Will Experience

The Albright International Leaders Program offers a comprehensive leadership development experience that extends far beyond traditional training programs.

1. Leadership Seminar

The program begins with an in-person kick-off event in Washington, D.C. Participants engage in an Aspen leadership seminar designed to encourage deep reflection, meaningful conversations, and authentic connections among cohort members.

This seminar-based learning model emphasizes dialogue rather than lectures, allowing participants to exchange perspectives and learn from one another’s experiences.

2. Engagement with Global Leaders

Throughout the year, participants meet regularly with distinguished leaders from government, diplomacy, academia, business, media, and international organizations.

These engagements provide invaluable insights into global affairs, policymaking, international security, and emerging geopolitical challenges.

3. Exclusive Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a powerful international network that includes:

  • Senior government officials
  • Members of Congress
  • Diplomats
  • Journalists
  • Academic experts
  • Business leaders
  • Aspen Strategy Group members
  • Alumni of Aspen leadership programs

These connections can create long-term opportunities for collaboration, mentorship, and professional advancement.

4. Aspen Security Forum Participation

One of the highlights of the program is participation in the Aspen Security Forum in Aspen, Colorado.

The Aspen Security Forum is one of the most respected gatherings in the world for discussions on national security, foreign policy, diplomacy, defense, and international relations. Participants gain direct exposure to leading voices shaping global policy.

5. Alumni and Cross-Program Events

The program also provides opportunities to engage with alumni from previous cohorts and selected events involving participants from the Aspen Strategy Group’s Rising Leaders Program.

These interactions help strengthen professional networks across sectors and national boundaries.

Benefits of the Program

The Albright International Leaders Program offers numerous benefits, including:

  • Access to high-level policymakers and decision-makers.
  • Leadership development through Aspen seminars.
  • Professional growth and skill enhancement.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge discussions on global affairs.
  • Participation in the renowned Aspen Security Forum.
  • Networking with leaders from around the world.
  • Lifelong membership in an influential alumni community.
  • No participation fee for selected candidates.

The program is designed to help participants become more effective leaders while expanding their influence and impact within their respective fields.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the Albright International Leaders Program, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a non-U.S. citizen or green card holder.
  • Be between 35 and 45 years old.
  • Possess a minimum of 10 years of professional experience.
  • Demonstrate professional involvement in diplomacy, international affairs, international development, human rights, security, global economics, or related fields.
  • Have at least C1-level English proficiency.
  • Be working and residing in the United States throughout the entire duration of the program.

The program is particularly suited for professionals affiliated with embassies, multilateral institutions, international organizations, and related sectors.

Required Application Materials

Applicants will be required to submit:

  • A letter of recommendation.
  • A brief statement of interest.
  • A short-answer essay.
  • A resume limited to two pages.

Applications will be reviewed by the Aspen Strategy Group Selection Committee.

Selection Process

The selection process includes:

Application Review

All submitted materials will undergo a rigorous evaluation by the selection committee.

Interview Stage

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in a brief interview, typically lasting approximately 15 minutes.

Final Selection

Successful applicants will be notified of their admission status and expected to commit fully to the program’s activities and requirements.

Important Dates

Applications Open: June 2027

Selection Notifications: September 2027

Program Kick-Off: October 2027

Washington, D.C. Programming: December 2027

Aspen Security Forum Graduation: July 2028

Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials early.

Why You Should Apply

The Albright International Leaders Program represents a rare opportunity for accomplished international professionals to expand their leadership capacity while engaging with some of the most influential thinkers and decision-makers in global affairs.

By combining leadership development, policy engagement, networking, and international collaboration, the program creates an environment where emerging global leaders can learn, grow, and contribute meaningfully to solving complex international challenges.

For professionals seeking to strengthen their impact in diplomacy, international development, security, or global governance, the Albright International Leaders Program offers an unparalleled platform for advancement.

Deadline: Not yet announced.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities ,click here

The post Aspen Albright International Leaders Program 2028: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for International Professionals in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding)
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change has officially announced a competitive and highly significant call for applications for the Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Mental Health and Climate Change. This initiative is designed to support the development of a scientifically rigorous indicator that will track and monitor the mental health impacts […]

The post Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change has officially announced a competitive and highly significant call for applications for the Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Mental Health and Climate Change. This initiative is designed to support the development of a scientifically rigorous indicator that will track and monitor the mental health impacts of climate change-related hazards globally.

This award is established in honour of Professor Tord Envold Kjellström (1948–2025), a globally respected environmental and occupational health scientist whose pioneering research significantly advanced understanding of climate-related health risks, including occupational heat exposure and environmental change impacts.

The selected expert or expert team will collaborate closely with the Lancet Countdown team to design an indicator that meets strict scientific criteria and contributes to global climate-health monitoring systems.

Objective of the Award

The primary goal of this opportunity is to develop a robust and validated indicator capable of measuring how climate change influences mental health outcomes. This indicator will become part of the Lancet Countdown’s annual monitoring framework, contributing to global policy and scientific understanding.

The work will focus on:

  • Developing a scientifically valid mental health–climate change indicator
  • Ensuring methodological rigor aligned with Lancet Countdown indicator standards
  • Collaborating with global experts in climate health monitoring
  • Supporting integration into annual global reporting systems
  • Advancing evidence-based climate and mental health policy

Funding Details

This award provides substantial research support for the selected applicant(s):

  • Funding amount: Up to £100,000 per award
  • Funding duration: 6 to 18 months
  • Number of awards: One
  • Co-applicants: Eligible
  • Funding structure: One-off award
  • Host requirements: Must be eligible to receive funding from Wellcome

Key Dates and Timeline

Applicants must carefully note the official timeline:

  • Applications open: 1 June 2026 at 08:00 BST
  • Application deadline: 12 July 2026 at 17:00 BST
  • Duration of project: 6–18 months following award allocation

Late submissions will not be considered.

Application Process

Interested applicants are expected to:

  1. Review the full Guidance for Applicants provided by Lancet Countdown
  2. Download and complete the official application pack
  3. Submit applications via the designated Qualtrics submission platform once applications open
  4. Participate in or review the information webinar for further clarification on requirements

Applicants are encouraged to ensure that their proposals align closely with the Lancet Countdown’s scientific framework and indicator methodology.

For further inquiries, applicants may contact:

  • Email: info@lancetcountdown.org

Expected Outputs

Successful applicants will be responsible for producing:

  • A validated mental health and climate change indicator
  • Scientific documentation supporting methodology and implementation
  • Collaboration outputs with Lancet Countdown research teams
  • Contribution to global annual climate-health reporting

Importance of This Award

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major driver of mental health challenges, including anxiety, trauma, displacement stress, and disaster-related psychological impacts. However, global standardized indicators to measure these effects remain limited.

This award addresses that gap by supporting innovation in:

  • Climate-sensitive mental health surveillance
  • Global health indicator development
  • Policy-relevant climate-health data systems
  • Evidence-based climate resilience strategies

Eligibility Summary (Separate Section)

Who Can Apply

  • Individual experts or expert teams
  • Researchers in mental health, epidemiology, climate science, public health, or related fields
  • Applicants with strong experience in indicator development or health metrics
  • Institutions capable of receiving Wellcome funding

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to international applicants
  • Applicants from all countries and continents may apply, provided institutional eligibility for funding is met

Required Expertise Areas

  • Mental health research
  • Climate change and environmental health
  • Epidemiology or biostatistics
  • Public health indicators and data systems
  • Health systems research

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Award Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications Open for the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme African artificial intelligence startups now have a unique opportunity to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities, improve operational resilience, and prepare for sustainable growth through the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme. The Cyber4Africa Programme is a collaborative initiative delivered by the AI Hub for […]

The post AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme

African artificial intelligence startups now have a unique opportunity to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities, improve operational resilience, and prepare for sustainable growth through the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme.

The Cyber4Africa Programme is a collaborative initiative delivered by the AI Hub for Sustainable Development, Cyber 4.0, and Cisco Kenya. Designed specifically for Africa’s growing AI ecosystem, the programme aims to equip startups with the cybersecurity knowledge, technical support, and operational tools required to scale securely in increasingly complex digital environments.

As artificial intelligence solutions become more deeply integrated into critical sectors such as healthcare, financial services, government technology, energy systems, and digital infrastructure, cybersecurity has become a fundamental requirement for sustainable innovation. The Cyber4Africa Programme addresses this need by helping startups build security and trust into their products and operations from the beginning.

About the AI Hub for Sustainable Development

The AI Hub for Sustainable Development is a G7-endorsed initiative jointly led by the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy and the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) under the Italy-Africa Mattei Plan.

The Hub was established to foster transformative AI innovation across Africa by supporting entrepreneurs, innovators, startups, and ecosystem builders. Working with more than 130 innovators across 18 countries, the initiative seeks to accelerate AI-driven development and create solutions that address some of the continent’s most pressing challenges.

The Hub focuses on strengthening three key pillars:

  • Green compute infrastructure
  • Data ecosystems
  • Talent development

Through strategic partnerships involving African countries, Italy, the European Union, and G7 stakeholders, the Hub is creating an ecosystem where innovators can access resources, partnerships, and opportunities needed to build impactful AI solutions.

What is the Cyber4Africa Programme?

Cyber4Africa extends the broader AI Hub ecosystem by focusing specifically on cybersecurity and digital trust.

The programme recognizes that many promising AI startups face growing security challenges as they scale. These challenges include:

  • Data protection vulnerabilities
  • Cybersecurity threats
  • Infrastructure security risks
  • Regulatory compliance requirements
  • Operational security weaknesses
  • Trust and safety concerns within AI systems

To address these issues, Cyber4Africa helps startups integrate cybersecurity principles throughout the AI development lifecycle. The programme promotes a “safety-by-design” approach that embeds security considerations into products, platforms, and services from the earliest stages of development.

By participating in the programme, startups can strengthen their resilience while increasing investor confidence, customer trust, and readiness for expansion into new markets.

Programme Objectives

The Cyber4Africa Programme seeks to:

  1. Improve cybersecurity awareness among African AI startups.
  2. Strengthen digital resilience across emerging AI businesses.
  3. Enhance data protection and privacy practices.
  4. Support secure infrastructure development.
  5. Enable safer deployment of AI technologies.
  6. Foster sustainable growth within Africa’s AI ecosystem.
  7. Build trust and security as foundational elements of innovation.

Key Benefits for Selected Startups

Selected participants will gain access to structured support designed to improve both technical and organizational cybersecurity readiness.

Benefits include:

1. Cyber Resilience and Awareness

Participants will receive support aimed at strengthening organizational cybersecurity awareness and preparedness.

Areas covered may include:

  • Cyber risk identification
  • Security best practices
  • Threat awareness
  • Incident preparedness
  • Security governance

2. Training and Capacity Building

Startups will benefit from specialized cybersecurity training designed to improve internal capabilities and strengthen long-term resilience.

Training may help teams:

  • Understand emerging cyber threats
  • Improve security management practices
  • Build secure AI systems
  • Enhance operational security procedures
  • Develop stronger compliance frameworks

3. Technical and Operational Support

The programme provides practical assistance to help startups address cybersecurity challenges affecting their products and operations.

Support may include:

  • Security assessments
  • Technical guidance
  • Infrastructure security recommendations
  • Data protection support
  • Operational resilience strategies

4. Access to Industry Expertise

Participants become part of a wider ecosystem involving:

  • AI Hub partners
  • Cybersecurity experts
  • Industry leaders
  • Innovation ecosystem stakeholders
  • Public and private sector collaborators

5. Specialized Training in Kenya

A unique benefit is available to Kenya-based innovators.

Eligible startups from Kenya will receive access to on-site training opportunities at the Cisco Cybersecurity Training Centre located in Nairobi, Kenya.

This provides hands-on learning experiences and exposure to industry-standard cybersecurity practices.

Priority Sectors

While startups from various sectors may apply, priority consideration is given to organizations operating in:

  • Healthcare
  • Financial Technology (Fintech)
  • Government Technology (GovTech)
  • Energy
  • AI Infrastructure

These sectors are particularly important because of their growing reliance on AI systems and their increased exposure to cybersecurity risks.

Why Cybersecurity Matters for AI Startups

Artificial intelligence solutions increasingly process large amounts of sensitive data and support critical decision-making processes.

Without adequate cybersecurity protections, startups may face:

  • Data breaches
  • Financial losses
  • Regulatory penalties
  • Service disruptions
  • Reputational damage
  • Loss of customer trust

The Cyber4Africa Programme helps startups proactively address these risks while positioning themselves for sustainable and secure growth.

Countries Represented in the Programme Ecosystem

The application process specifically recognizes startups operating across numerous African countries, including:

  • Algeria
  • Angola
  • Côte d’Ivoire
  • Democratic Republic of Congo
  • Egypt
  • Ethiopia
  • Gabon
  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Mauritania
  • Morocco
  • Mozambique
  • Republic of Congo
  • Rwanda
  • Senegal
  • Tanzania
  • Tunisia
  • Zambia

Startups operating in other African countries are also encouraged to apply where eligible.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: 8 June 2026 at 23:59 Central European Time (CET).

Interested startups should submit their applications before the deadline to ensure consideration.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

Africa’s AI ecosystem is expanding rapidly, creating exciting opportunities for innovators developing solutions that can transform industries and improve lives. However, sustainable growth requires strong cybersecurity foundations.

The AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme provides African AI startups with access to training, technical support, resilience-building resources, and expert guidance needed to strengthen cybersecurity readiness and scale confidently.

For startups operating in sectors where data protection, trust, and digital resilience are essential, this programme offers an opportunity to build stronger systems, safer products, and more sustainable pathways for growth.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Top Artificial Intelligence Safety, Governance, Security, and Policy Fellowships Open for Applications in 2026
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming societies, economies, governments, and global security systems. As AI capabilities continue to advance, there is growing demand for professionals who can help shape AI governance, strengthen AI safety, improve AI security, and ensure responsible deployment of powerful AI systems. Several prestigious fellowships and leadership programs are currently accepting applications for […]

The post Top Artificial Intelligence Safety, Governance, Security, and Policy Fellowships Open for Applications in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming societies, economies, governments, and global security systems. As AI capabilities continue to advance, there is growing demand for professionals who can help shape AI governance, strengthen AI safety, improve AI security, and ensure responsible deployment of powerful AI systems.

Several prestigious fellowships and leadership programs are currently accepting applications for 2026 cohorts. These opportunities provide funding, mentorship, networking, research support, career development, and direct engagement with leading experts working at the forefront of artificial intelligence policy, governance, security, and safety.

This article highlights some of the most significant opportunities currently available to professionals, researchers, students, engineers, policymakers, and emerging leaders seeking to contribute to the future of AI.

1. Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects

The BASE Fellowship is a 13-week remote programme running from September 2026 through December 2026. The initiative is specifically designed to support and develop Black talent in critical AI-related fields that are shaping the future of technology and society.

Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI.

The programme is seeking 3–4 fellows for its current cohort and offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world’s leading centres for AI ethics research.

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative of the Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI. The programme was created in response to the growing ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and adoption of artificial intelligence technologies across society.

Unlike traditional research fellowships that focus primarily on academic outputs, this programme emphasizes real-world impact. It seeks to support projects that can shape the development of AI in ethical and socially beneficial directions while extending the influence of research already taking place within the Institute.

The fellowship is grounded in:

  • Philosophical inquiry
  • Academic independence
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Practical impact
  • Responsible innovation
  • Apply Here 

2. BASE Fellowship Fall 2026

The BASE Fellowship is a 13-week remote programme running from September 2026 through December 2026. The initiative is specifically designed to support and develop Black talent in critical AI-related fields that are shaping the future of technology and society.

The programme focuses on three major areas:

  1. AI Safety
  2. AI Governance
  3. AI Security

Through structured mentorship, collaborative learning, and practical research projects, fellows gain the knowledge and experience necessary to contribute meaningfully to the AI safety ecosystem and emerging governance discussions worldwide.

The fellowship aims to ensure that the Black diaspora remains actively involved in shaping the development and regulation of advanced AI systems rather than being excluded from these transformative conversations.

Mission of Black in AI Safety and Ethics (BASE)

Black in AI Safety and Ethics was established to create a connected and informed Black community within the fields of AI safety, governance, and security.

The organisation’s mission includes:

  • Increasing Black representation in AI leadership
  • Supporting Black researchers and practitioners
  • Promoting ethical and equitable AI systems
  • Building a global network of Black AI experts
  • Preventing the marginalisation of Black communities in the age of artificial intelligence
  • Ensuring fairness, accountability, and collective well-being in future AI systems

As artificial intelligence increasingly influences economies, governance, communication, employment, and public life, BASE seeks to guarantee that Black voices remain influential in shaping AI policy and technological development. Apply here  

The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is now accepting applications from Black researchers, professionals, students, and practitioners interested in advancing their expertise in AI Safety, AI Governance, and AI Security. Designed as a fully remote and part-time fellowship, the programme offers a unique opportunity for members of the Black diaspora to gain hands-on research experience, expert mentorship, and foundational training in some of the world’s most important and rapidly evolving fields in artificial intelligence.

Organised by Black in AI Safety and Ethics, the fellowship seeks to increase Black representation and leadership within the global AI ecosystem while ensuring that future AI systems are safe, equitable, accountable, and human-centered.

Applications for the BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme close on 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth (AOE).

3. Talos Network Artificial Intelligence Policy Leaders Programme 2026

The Talos Network Artificial Intelligence Policy Leaders Programme is designed for experienced professionals who are ready to influence the future of AI governance in Europe.

The programme aims to strengthen Europe’s capacity to govern advanced AI systems safely and effectively by supporting professionals with extensive experience in policy, government, international organizations, academia, industry, and related sectors.

Programme Tracks

Placement Track

The Placement Track is intended for professionals with at least eight years of experience who want to transition into AI governance.

Participants receive:

  • A 12-month paid placement at an AI-focused organization
  • Base compensation of EUR 5,000 per month for Brussels-based placements
  • Cost-of-living adjustments for placements in other cities
  • Career transition support
  • Mentorship and networking opportunities

Training Track

The Training Track targets senior professionals with at least twelve years of experience who want to incorporate AI governance and AI safety considerations into their current roles.

Participants receive:

  • Advanced AI governance training
  • Strategic positioning support
  • Access to high-level policy networks
  • Career coaching and mentorship

Programme Components

Virtual European AI Policy Bootcamp

  • Dates: 10 September – 22 October 2026
  • Weekly sessions every Thursday
  • Approximately 10 hours per week
  • Compatible with full-time employment

Policymaking Summit
